Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0x2100 "SPRITEMAN-2100" -Di_irq_exit=1 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Sprite Man.prg"
Metadata.Notes:
This game consists of two BASIC programs, the first to load the game, and then the game itself, which is auto-run once loaded.
Therefore, to dump the game, set a breakpoint on $A7AE after the don't press stop on the tape message disappears and the screen goes white.
To avoid running the slow BASIC statements from lines 5 to 25 of the game program every time it is started, the following steps were performed:
1. Removed the breakpoint on $A7AE when hit 2. Changed the byte at $0824 from $97 to $8F (this disables the POKE 771,0 instruction on line 5 by changing it to a REM so that the execution address of the BASIC idle loop at $0302/$0303 is left untouched) 3. Changed the two bytes at $08FC from $97, $35 to $90, $3A (this places a STOP instruction at the start of line 100) 4. Resumed the program and wait for the program to break at line 100 and the ready prompt is displayed 5. Changed the two bytes at $08FC back to $97, $35 to restore the original POKE instruction at the start of line 100 6. The game was then dumped as normal 7. A new routine was placed at $2100 which sets up the correct VICII settings and necessary BASIC pointers and runs the game program. The pointer to the start of the BASIC program ($2B/$2C) is set to $08F8 in order to start the program at line 100 which skips the slow statements from lines 5 to 25. 8. This routine also disables the RUN/STOP key by modifying the STOP vector by setting $E1 at $0328
